notes: R-mode: /search accepts GET with ?q= parameter, renders a search form at /search (no q), and renders a Results page for any non-empty q.
E-mode: Every possible query (broad, narrow, single char, multi-word) returns the identical 235-byte 'No results found' response with HTTP 200. POST /search returns 404. /api/search returns 404.
The cart page is reachable at /cart (200) and shows a checkout button pointing to POST /cart/checkout.

No query-term reflection in results page body — the response is identical regardless of query input, suggesting the search backend is completely broken (empty catalog, broken DB connection, or similar).

observations.value:
GET /search?q= always returns HTTP 200 with body containing only '

No results found.

' — no results are ever returned regardless of query. Tested with: 'widget', 'apple', 'gizmo', 'foo', 'a', 'the', 'acme', 'product', 'test+item'. Every query yields the same 235-byte 'No results found' response, indicating the search index/data source is broken or disconnected.

GET /search?q=<term> response body never echoes the submitted query term back to the user. A working search results page would typically display the search term (e.g. 'Results for: widget'); this reflection is absent across all tested queries.
